segments.title: Inside the former Kure Naval Station commander's residence
segments.summary: An on-location look at Irifuneyama Memorial Museum, the former official residence of the commander-in-chief at Kure Naval Station.
segments.body: Clarke visits Irifuneyama Memorial Museum in Kure and identifies the building as the former official residence of the commander-in-chief of Kure Naval Station. He presents the house as a place shaped by important conversations and decisions, rather than simply a preserved domestic interior. The stop gives visitors a sense of the institutional setting behind a major naval station and its senior command. It also places the museum within a day of naval-history visits that includes the Yamato Museum annex and other Kure museums.

evidence.note: Clarke identifies Irifuneyama as the former commander-in-chief's residence at Kure Naval Station and outlines the day's museum visits.
